_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Corrective Surgery for foot deformities (Vertical Talus)
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 5 years' experience

preauth.label: Clinical notes with planned line of Surgery/Treatment
X-ray/MRI labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part

claim.label: Post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Detailed Clinical notes – all vitals, history signs & symptoms, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, advise for admission, and planned line of treatment?
Was the X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) submitted?
Was the clinical photograph of affected part submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Was the Detailed Procedure/Operative notes submitted?
Was the post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) submitted?
Was the Post Procedure clinical photograph submitted?
Is the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes do not include all vitals, history, signs & symptoms, physical examination, indication for procedure, admission advice, or planned line of treatment.
X-ray/MRI report (pre- or post-procedure) is not labelled with patient ID, date, or affected side (Left/Right).
Clinical photograph of the affected part (pre- or post-procedure) is missing.
Detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details are not available at claim submission.
Procedure/Operative notes are missing or incomplete.
Clinical notes and X-ray reports are not indicative of the requested procedure.

clinical_key_pointers: Is a rigid flat foot deformity characterized by fixed hind foot equinus and an irreducible talonavicular dislocation.
Hall mark is Dorsal subluxation/dislocation of navicular, Equinus talus, Equinus and valgus of calcaneus and foot.
Bony changes include Talus- vertical, articular cartilage present over dorsum of talar head. Calcaneum- lateral translation, valgus.
